2604.09489.title: XFED: Non-Collusive Model Poisoning Attack Against Byzantine-Robust Federated Classifiers
2604.09489.paper_summary: Model poisoning attacks pose a significant security threat to Federated Learning (FL). Most existing model poisoning attacks rely on collusion, requiring adversarial clients to coordinate by exchanging local benign models and synchronizing the generation of their poisoned updates. However, sustaining such coordination is increasingly impractical in real-world FL deployments, as it effectively requires botnet-like control over many devices. This approach is costly to maintain and highly vulnerable to detection. This context raises a fundamental question: Can model poisoning attacks remain effective without any communication between attackers? To address this challenge, we introduce and formalize the \textbf{non-collusive attack model}, in which all compromised clients share a common adversarial objective but operate independently. Under this model, each attacker generates its malicious update without communicating with other adversaries, accessing other clients' updates, or relying on any knowledge of server-side defenses. To demonstrate the feasibility of this threat model, we propose \textbf{XFED}, the first aggregation-agnostic, non-collusive model poisoning attack. Our empirical evaluation across six benchmark datasets shows that XFED bypasses eight state-of-the-art defenses and outperforms six existing model poisoning attacks. These findings indicate that FL systems are substantially less secure than previously believed and underscore the urgent need for more robust and practical defense mechanisms.
2604.09489.paper_summary_zh: 模型中毒攻擊對聯邦學習（FL）構成了重大安全威脅。大多數現有的模型中毒攻擊依賴於共謀，要求對抗客戶端通過交換本地良性模型和同步生成其中毒更新來協調。然而，在現實世界的 FL 部署中，維持這種協調變得越來越不切實際，因為這實際上需要對許多設備進行類似僵尸網絡的控制。這種方法維護成本高昂，且極易被檢測。這一背景提出了一個根本性問題：在攻擊者之間沒有任何通信的情況下，模型中毒攻擊能否保持有效？為了解決這一挑戰，我們引入並正式化了 \textbf{非共謀攻擊模型}，在這個模型中，所有受損客戶端共享一個共同的對抗目標，但獨立運作。在這個模型下，每個攻擊者在不與其他對手通信、不訪問其他客戶端的更新或不依賴於任何伺服器端防禦知識的情況下生成其惡意更新。為了證明這一威脅模型的可行性，我們提出了 \textbf{XFED}，這是第一個聚合無關的非共謀模型中毒攻擊。我們在六個基準數據集上的實證評估顯示，XFED 繞過了八種最先進的防禦，並超越了六種現有的模型中毒攻擊。這些發現表明，FL 系統的安全性遠低於先前的認知，並強調了對更強大和實用的防禦機制的迫切需求。
2604.09489.author: Israt Jahan Mouri et.al.
2604.09489.authors: Israt Jahan Mouri, Muhammad Ridowan, Muhammad Abdullah Adnan
